abstract: Conditionally-automated vehicles require drivers to remain hazard-aware while disengaged from driving and engaged in non-driving related tasks (NDRTs), yet conventional visual warning interfaces do not fully address this attentional conflict. While psychological research demonstrates social cues are processed more efficiently than visual information, this has not been evaluated for safety-critical warnings in conditionally-automated driving. This paper presents the first empirical investigation into using a virtual agent to indicate danger and aid situational awareness while driver perform a NDRT. Across two experiments, hazard prediction ability was assessed with either a visual cue or virtual agent cueing danger. When including an attention-capturing element, both visual and social cue types mitigated driver distraction, maintaining hazard prediction comparable with undistracted driving. These findings demonstrate that virtual agents can be as effective as conventional visual cues in aiding situational awareness in safety-critical conditionally-automated driving contexts, offering design insights for in-vehicle attentional displays supporting driver awareness.

ur.ai_reason: 虚拟Agent在自动驾驶中 cue danger；含用户实验但核心是驾驶注意力机制
ur.tldr: 在条件自动驾驶场景中，驾驶员需在从事非驾驶任务时保持对危险的感知。研究首次实验比较虚拟代理（社交提示）与传统视觉提示在提示危险时的效果，发现加入注意力捕捉元素后，两种提示均能有效缓解分心，保持与未分心驾驶相当的危险预测准确率。结果表明虚拟代理在提升情境感知方面具有与视觉提示同等效能，为车内注意力显示设计提供参考。

title: The effects of using a Virtual Agent to Signal Danger on Hazard Prediction ability in Conditionally-Automated Driving
title_zh: 虚拟代理信号危险对条件自动化驾驶危险预测能力的影响
